@benmschmidt @rbthisted I am taking a long time to say something simple; I much prefer the meta-skill of "Learning Something New" over the specific skill of Computer Science, or even worse, hyper-specific CS like a specific technology or language- gimme the self-driven learner over the expert any day

@benmschmidt @rbthisted I really don't think you need to know how to code anymore. You need to know code *literacy* like for loops, if/then- but most kids get that from playing with redstone in Minecraft when they're 6, and frankly the solutions in the real world not dissimilar

@benmschmidt @rbthisted I mean, it's dramatically changed in the past 6 months for me. I have HS students I am "teaching" basic javascript in combination with a GPT-4 discord bot; they want to do a project, I ask the robot the magic words, and off they go - BUT, that is only possible w/ self-driven kids

@benmschmidt @rbthisted Some of my favorite dataviz practitioners come from other fields, music, science, journalism- and used that existing knowledge to push dataviz further in new directions, new syntheses and approaches;  I would prefer a curious self-driven person with world-knowledge over a "coder"

@benmschmidt @rbthisted I think one of the crucial missing things in this conversation for me isn't CS expertise, it's the ability to be curious and *self-driven* - if you have proven your ability to learn, explore, and create in a new field that's all I need, code is just another medium

@benmschmidt @rbthisted the most successful teams and companies I have seen and been on raise their young, and through doing so make the product better through teaching & access to beginner's mind - and the team benefits from deeply shared experience and learning/growing *together*

@benmschmidt @rbthisted I have seen two different fields (dataviz and AI) emerge before education tracks to support them did- if we are all basically on the same footing, I'd much prefer to learn alongside / train someone junior who has an understanding of the world and how the product fits into it
